mnemonics: ESSENTIAL: Engineers Structure Software, Tackling All Needs, Logically
keyPoints: Software is a logical system element, not a physical one.
Software is engineered, not manufactured; costs are concentrated in design, not production.
Software doesn't 'wear out' but it can deteriorate due to changing environments and requirements (software aging).
commonMistakes: Confusing software engineering with traditional engineering manufacturing processes.
Assuming that because software doesn't physically break, it doesn't require maintenance.
Underestimating the importance of rigorous design and requirements analysis.
